revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''四十塚古墳群'''（しじゅうづかこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[深谷市]]にある[[古墳群]]。 「四十坂古墳群」とも呼ば...'
revision.text:
'''四十塚古墳群'''（しじゅうづかこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[深谷市]]にある[[古墳群]]。
「四十坂古墳群」とも呼ばれている。古くから遺物が掘り出され、昭和7年に四十塚古墳を発掘した際には横矧板鋲留短甲1、銅製五鈴鏡板付轡1、鉄製楕円形鏡板付轡1、鉄斧1が出土した。これらの出土品は平成15年に県指定有形文化財に指定されている。かつては多くの古墳が存在したが、現在墳丘の残っている古墳は浅間塚古墳と[[寅稲荷古墳]]の2基のみである。
== 参考文献 == 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 大里』 さきたま出版会、2004年
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ししゆうつかこふんくん}} [[category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[category:深谷市の歴史]]

== 参考文献 == * 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 大里』 さきたま出版会、2004年

「四十坂古墳群」とも呼ばれている。古くから遺物が掘り出され、1932年(昭和7年)に四十塚古墳を発掘した際には横矧板鋲留短甲1、銅製五鈴鏡板付轡1、鉄製楕円形鏡板付轡1、鉄斧1が出土した。これらの出土品は2003年(平成15年)に県指定有形文化財に指定されている。かつては多くの古墳が存在したが、現在墳丘の残っている古墳は[[四十坂浅間山古墳|浅間山古墳]]と[[寅稲荷古墳]]の2基のみである。

{{DEFAULTSORT:ししゆうつかこふんくん}} [[category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[category:深谷市の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{単一の出典|date=2013年10月21日 (月) 07:26 (UTC)}} '''四十塚古墳群'''（しじゅうづかこふんぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[深谷市]]にある[[古墳群]]。
「四十坂古墳群」とも呼ばれている。「四十塚」の名の通りかつては多くの古墳が存在し、古くから多くの遺物が掘り出されたが、現在墳丘の残っている古墳は[[四十坂浅間山古墳|浅間山古墳]]と[[寅稲荷古墳]]の2基のみである。
== 主な古墳 == *四十塚古墳 :大型の円墳と推定される古墳で、昭和7年に発掘されている。主体部は自然石で構成された舟形礫槨で、長さ3.6メートル・幅1.8メートルほどが残存していた。副葬品は、横矧板鋲留短甲1、銅製五鈴鏡板付轡1、鉄製楕円形鏡板付轡1、鉄斧1である。銅製五鈴鏡板付轡は全国的に見ても出土数が少なく、東日本では初めてである。5世紀後半の築造。出土品は平成15年3月18日付けで県指定有形文化財に指定された。
*[[四十坂浅間山古墳|浅間山古墳]] :径35メートル・高さ4メートルの円墳。昭和54年市指定史跡。
*[[寅稲荷古墳]] :全長51メートルの前方後円墳。昭和54年市指定有形文化財。
== 参考文献 == * 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 大里』pp444-468、さきたま出版会、2004年
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.fukaya.saitama.jp/syougaigakusyu/web_rekisi_bunkazai/prefecture/prefectual01_yukei.htm 深谷市内の埼玉県指定有形文化財] - 深谷市ホームページ
{{DEFAULTSORT:ししゆうつかこふんくん}} [[category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[category:深谷市の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.maniwa.or.jp/web/?c=spot-2&pk=32 Maniwa Tourist Information home page] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.okayama-kanko.jp/spot/11338 Okayama Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]]

[[ja:四ツ塚古墳群]]

revision.comment: Reverted edits by [[Special:Contributions/Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) to last revision by [[User:wikipedia>Happyecheveria|wikipedia>Happyecheveria]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.maniwa.or.jp/web/?c=spot-2&pk=32 Maniwa Tourist Information home page] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.okayama-kanko.jp/spot/11338 Okayama Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.maniwa.or.jp/web/?c=spot-2&pk=32 Maniwa Tourist Information home page] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.okayama-kanko.jp/spot/11338 Okayama Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages without Japanese interwiki]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:I hope this works]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:I hope this works]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster[[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages without Japanese interwiki]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:I hope this works]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:I hope this works]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster[[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages without Japanese interwiki]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:I hope this works]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:I hope this works]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster[[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun clusters]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. {{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.maniwa.or.jp/web/?c=spot-2&pk=32 Maniwa Tourist Information home page] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.okayama-kanko.jp/spot/11338 Okayama Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:I hope this works]][[fr:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:I hope this works]][[fr:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:I hope this works]][[fr:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:I hope this works]][[fr:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:I hope this works]][[fr:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:I hope this works]][[fr:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[ja:{{citation needed|Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster}}[[ja:四十塚古墳群]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
