{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small burial mounds built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small Kofuns built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small Kofuns built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small Kofuns built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small Kofuns built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small Kofuns built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small Kofuns built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue ware]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small Kofuns built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.maniwa.or.jp/web/?c=spot-2&pk=32 Maniwa Tourist Information home page] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.okayama-kanko.jp/spot/11338 Okayama Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[fr:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[fr:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[fr:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[fr:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[fr:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[fr:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]] <!--Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster-->[[Category:11]][[sn:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[ja:{{citation needed|Yotsuzuka Kofun Cluster}}[[ja:四十塚古墳群]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maniwa, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small Kofuns built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small Kofuns built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四十塚古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17211755]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa, Okayama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small Kofuns built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[:en:Asahi River|Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[:en:Chūgoku Mountains|Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.maniwa.or.jp/web/?c=spot-2&pk=32 Maniwa Tourist Information home page] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.okayama-kanko.jp/spot/11338 Okayama Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small Kofuns built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[:en:Asahi River|Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[:en:Chūgoku Mountains|Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small Kofuns built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[:en:Asahi River|Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[:en:Chūgoku Mountains|Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.maniwa.or.jp/web/?c=spot-2&pk=32 Maniwa Tourist Information home page] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.okayama-kanko.jp/spot/11338 Okayama Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四十塚古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17211755]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa, Okayama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small Kofuns built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[:en:Asahi River|Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[:en:Chūgoku Mountains|Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.maniwa.or.jp/web/?c=spot-2&pk=32 Maniwa Tourist Information home page] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.okayama-kanko.jp/spot/11338 Okayama Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}

More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四十塚古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四十塚古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:四十塚古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17211755]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Maniwa, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | native_name =四ツ塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa, Okayama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|49|N|133|43|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster'''|四ツ塚古墳群| Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Hiruzen Kaminaga neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Maniwa, Okayama|Maniwa]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 1929.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163591|title=四ツ塚古墳群|trans-title= Yotsuzuka Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster consists of 16 large and small Kofuns built on a terrace on the left bank of the [[:en:Asahi River|Asahi River]] in the Hiruzen Basin of northern Okayama surrounded by the [[:en:Chūgoku Mountains|Chūgoku Mountains]]. More than 170 ''kofun'' have been identified in the Hiruzen Basin in total, among which the four {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} in the Yotsuzuka Kofun cluster are the largest. Per an [[archaeological excavation]], Yotsuzuka No.1 was determined to be 24 meters in diameter, and to have a side-entry type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] orientated to the north. More than 100 [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were found, including beads, iron products, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. These excavated items are now stored in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] and date the tumulus to the late Kofun period, or around AD 500. During an excavation of the Yotsuzuka No.13 tumulus (which is not part of the National Historic Site), large numbers of chicken and house-shaped ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were recovered. These artifacts are stored and exhibited at the Hiruzen Folk Museum.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Yotsuzuka Historic Site Park has been developed around the 16 remaining tumuli and is located about 15 minutes by car from Yonago Expressway Hiruzen IC.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.maniwa.or.jp/web/?c=spot-2&pk=32 Maniwa Tourist Information home page] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.okayama-kanko.jp/spot/11338 Okayama Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}
